title: Dryopteris pallida
intro: Dryopteris pallida, also known as the pale male fern, is a species of fern native to Europe and western Asia. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Dryopteris pallida include sesquiterpene lactones, phenolic compounds, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
taxonomy: Dryopteris pallida is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. The genus Dryopteris is part of the division Pteridophyta. The classification of Dryopteris pallida reflects its evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic makeup.
